name: search_memories
description:
Token-aware full-text search across titles and bodies.

The query is split into tokens on non-alphanumeric boundaries (so `russh CVE`
becomes [russh, CVE]). In default `mode: 'all'` (recommended) every token
must appear somewhere in the memory's title or body — this is the precision
mode and the right default when you're trying to recall a specific thing.

Switch to `mode: 'any'` for broad exploratory queries when you'd rather see
loosely related memories than risk a zero-hit reply.

Scoring: +5 per token found in title, +1 per body occurrence (capped at 5
per token), +3 if the title contains the verbatim query as a substring.
Ties broken by recency. Snippet shows merged context windows around the
matched terms (max ~400 chars).

query.description: Search terms. Tokenized on non-alphanumeric boundaries; multi-word queries are AND-matched by default.

mode.description: 'all' (default, precise) requires every token; 'any' (broad) returns memories matching any token.

cwd.description: Optional. Absolute path used to discover the hub by walking up for `.bridgememory/`. Defaults to the server's process cwd.
